annotations.label: Fibre category

anchor.snippet: is called the fibre category (or fibre ) over

annotations.note: `Functor.Fiber p S` is the category of objects mapping to `S` with morphisms lying over `𝟙 S`, exactly the fibre category.

annotations.label: Cartesian morphism

anchor.snippet: (or simply cartesian ) if it satisfies the following condition

annotations.note: Wikipedia's cartesian morphism (the lifting/factorization universal property, Stacks 02XK) is Mathlib's `IsStronglyCartesian`; `IsCartesian` is the weaker over-identity variant.

annotations.label: Inverse image

anchor.snippet: is called an inverse image of its projection

annotations.note: `pullbackObj` is the domain of a chosen cartesian lift of `f` over `a`, i.e. a chosen inverse image of `a`.

annotations.label: Cartesian morphisms of a fibre are isomorphisms

anchor.snippet: The cartesian morphisms of a fibre category

annotations.note: A strongly cartesian morphism over an iso is an iso; specializing the base to `𝟙` gives that cartesian morphisms within a fibre are isomorphisms.

annotations.label: Inverse images unique up to isomorphism

anchor.snippet: two such inverse images are isomorphic in

annotations.note: `domainUniqueUpToIso` gives the canonical isomorphism between the domains (inverse images) of two cartesian arrows over the same morphism.

annotations.label: S-category (category over S)

anchor.snippet: or a category over

annotations.note: `BasedCategory 𝒮` is a category equipped with a functor to `𝒮`, exactly a category over `S`.

annotations.label: S-categories form a 2-category

anchor.snippet: form in a natural manner a 2-category

annotations.note: `BasedCategory.bicategory` plus the `Bicategory.Strict` instance make categories over `𝒮` a strict bicategory, i.e. a 2-category.

annotations.label: Cartesian functor

anchor.snippet: is called a cartesian functor if it takes cartesian morphisms to cartesian morphisms

annotations.note: `BasedFunctor` (functor over `𝒮`) is formalized, but no class for a functor preserving cartesian morphisms exists.

annotations.label: Cartesian section

anchor.snippet: is called a cartesian section

annotations.note: No notion of a cartesian section of a fibred category is present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Section functor is fully faithful (Giraud Lemma 5.7)

anchor.snippet: is fully faithful (Lemma 5.7 of Giraud

annotations.note: The full faithfulness of the cartesian-section functor (Giraud) is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Fibred category

anchor.snippet: is a fibred category (or a fibred

annotations.note: `Functor.IsFibered p` formalizes a fibred category as in SGA 1 VI.6.1 (prefibered plus cartesian morphisms closed under composition).

annotations.label: Cartesian sections equivalent to fibre over terminal object

anchor.snippet: is an equivalence of categories and moreover surjective on objects

annotations.note: The equivalence between cartesian sections and the fibre over a terminal object is not formalized.

annotations.label: Cleavage and transport morphisms

anchor.snippet: is called a cleavage and the selected morphisms are called the transport morphisms

annotations.note: `pullbackMap` provides individually chosen cartesian lifts (transport morphisms) via choice, but there is no packaged `Cleavage` structure on a fibred category.

annotations.label: Cloven category

anchor.snippet: together with a cleavage is called a cloven category

annotations.note: Cloven-category data is equivalent to a pseudofunctor `𝒮ᵒᵖ ⥤ Cat`, which Mathlib formalizes, but the cloven-category structure on a fibred category is not defined directly.

annotations.label: Normalised cleavage

anchor.snippet: is called normalised if the transport morphisms include all identities in

annotations.note: There is no notion of a normalised cleavage (identities being among the transport morphisms) in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Compatibility isomorphism of inverse image functors

anchor.snippet: then there is an isomorphism of functors

annotations.note: The comparison iso `(f≫g)* ≅ g* ⋙ f*` is the pseudofunctor field `mapComp`, but it is given as data, not derived from a cleavage on a fibred category.

annotations.label: Cocycle compatibilities

anchor.snippet: These isomorphisms satisfy the following two compatibilities

annotations.note: The associativity/unit cocycle conditions are the pseudofunctor coherence axioms (`map₂_associator`, unitors), expressed as pseudofunctor laws rather than properties of a cleavage.

annotations.label: Reconstruction of cloven category from functors

anchor.snippet: satisfying the compatibilities above, defines a cloven category

annotations.note: The Grothendieck construction `∫ᶜ F` of a pseudofunctor yields `forget F`, which carries an `IsFibered` instance, reconstructing the cloven/fibred category from the functor data.

annotations.label: Splitting and split category

anchor.snippet: is called a splitting , and a fibred category with a splitting is called a split (fibred) category

annotations.note: A split category is the Grothendieck construction of a genuine functor to `Cat`, realized by `CategoryTheory.Grothendieck`, but a `splitting` structure on a fibred category is not defined.

annotations.label: Split categories correspond to true functors

anchor.snippet: correspond exactly to true functors from

annotations.note: `Grothendieck` builds a split fibration from a functor `C ⥤ Cat`, giving one direction, but the equivalence (correspondence) of split categories with true functors is not formalized.

annotations.label: Not all fibred categories admit splittings

anchor.snippet: not all fibred categories admit splittings

annotations.note: There is no Mathlib result that some fibred category admits no splitting.

annotations.label: Co-cartesian morphism

anchor.snippet: is called co-cartesian if it is cartesian for the opposite functor

annotations.note: `IsCocartesian`/`IsStronglyCocartesian` formalize co-cartesian morphisms via the dual universal property.

annotations.label: Co-fibred category

anchor.snippet: such that direct image exists for each morphism in

annotations.note: Co-cartesian morphisms are formalized, but there is no `IsCofibered`/opfibered class asserting direct images exist for every morphism.

annotations.label: Co-cleavage and co-splitting

anchor.snippet: A co-cleavage and a co-splitting are defined similarly

annotations.note: No co-cleavage or co-splitting notions exist in Mathlib.

annotations.label: 2-category of fibred categories

anchor.section: The 2-categories of fibred categories and split categories
anchor.snippet: The categories fibred over a fixed category

annotations.note: The bicategory of all categories over `𝒮` is formalized, but the sub-2-category of fibred categories (with cartesian functors) is not carved out separately.

annotations.label: 2-category of split categories

anchor.section: The 2-categories of fibred categories and split categories
anchor.snippet: Similarly the split categories over

annotations.note: The 2-category of split categories over `𝒮` is not formalized.

annotations.label: Forgetful 2-functor

anchor.section: The 2-categories of fibred categories and split categories
anchor.snippet: that simply forgets the splitting

annotations.note: Since splittings are not formalized, the forgetful 2-functor from split to fibred categories is absent.

annotations.label: Every fibred category is equivalent to a split category

anchor.snippet: each fibred category is in fact equivalent to a split category

annotations.note: The theorem that every fibred category is equivalent to a split one is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Left and right 2-adjoints to forgetful functor (Giraud)

anchor.snippet: admits a right 2-adjoint

annotations.note: Giraud's left/right 2-adjoints to the forgetful 2-functor are not formalized.

annotations.label: Adjunction functors are cartesian equivalences

anchor.snippet: are both cartesian and equivalences

annotations.note: No result that the relevant adjunction functors are cartesian equivalences exists.

annotations.label: Categories fibered in groupoids

anchor.snippet: called categories fibered in groupoids

annotations.note: There is no `fibered in groupoids` definition in Mathlib (the fibre categories being groupoids is not formalized).

annotations.label: Objects functor Cat to Set is a fibration

anchor.snippet: sending a category to its set of objects, is a fibration

annotations.note: The functor `Cat.objects : Cat ⥤ Type` exists, but it is not equipped with or proved to be a fibration.

annotations.label: Category of arrows

anchor.snippet: Categories of arrows : For any category

annotations.note: The arrow category `Arrow C` (as a comma category) exists, but its codomain fibration structure is not formalized.

annotations.label: Fibrations of graphs

anchor.snippet: Fibrations of graphs : Each directed graph

annotations.note: Fibrations of graphs as a fibred category are not formalized.

annotations.label: Fibre bundles

anchor.snippet: Fibre bundles : Fibre products exist in the category

annotations.note: Fibre bundles over Top as a fibred category (via pullback) are not formalized, though fibre bundles themselves exist elsewhere.

annotations.label: Vector bundles

anchor.snippet: Vector bundles : In a manner similar to the previous examples

annotations.note: Vector bundles as a fibred category are not formalized.

annotations.label: Sheaves on topological spaces

anchor.snippet: Sheaves on topological spaces : The inverse image functors of sheaves

annotations.note: The fibred-category structure on sheaves via inverse-image functors over Top is not formalized.

annotations.label: Sheaves on topoi

anchor.snippet: Sheaves on topoi : If

annotations.note: The fibred category of sheaves on topoi is not formalized.

annotations.label: Quasi-coherent sheaves on schemes

anchor.snippet: Quasi-coherent sheaves form a fibred category over the category of schemes

annotations.note: Quasi-coherent sheaves as a fibred category over schemes are not formalized.

annotations.label: Fibred category admitting no splitting

anchor.snippet: Fibred category admitting no splitting : A group

annotations.note: This counterexample of a fibred category with no splitting is not formalized.

annotations.label: Co-fibred category of sheaves

anchor.snippet: The direct image functor of sheaves makes the categories of sheaves on topological spaces into a co-fibred category

annotations.note: The co-fibred category of sheaves via direct-image functors is not formalized.

annotations.label: Groupoid objects internal to a category

anchor.snippet: comes from groupoid objects internal to a category

annotations.note: Internal groupoid objects (and their associated fibrations in groupoids) are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Group quotient [X/G]

anchor.snippet: Given a group object

annotations.note: The quotient stack `[X/G]` as a fibred category in groupoids is not formalized.

annotations.label: Two-term chain complex

anchor.snippet: For an abelian category

annotations.note: The fibred-in-groupoids example arising from a two-term chain complex is not formalized.
